content.text:
<user_query>
Missing "./dist/request-matcher/utils.js" specifier in "request-mocking-protocol" package

When I run the app locally, I'm getting this error. We should not be doing any mocking or using request mocking protocol. We should not be importing anything from dist when I'm running locally.

This is not how we want to mock out the authorization. We want to just hard code it for now and then load the page normally.

I'm not sure what's happened, but we should definitely fix this up so that it's a very idiomatic and normal working local tan stack start app. The only difference right now for local development is that we are hard coding the values for bypassing authentication and authorization and then loading the page normally.
</user_query>

Tracing where `request-mocking-protocol` and related mocking are used so we can remove them and implement a hardcoded local auth bypass.

[REDACTED]

Removing `request-mocking-protocol` from server code (static imports caused the broken `dist/` resolution). Using the existing in-memory stub whenever bypass auth is enabled, and simplifying Playwright tests to match the hardcoded fake data.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Here’s what was going wrong and what we changed.

### Cause
`src/server/org-api.ts` **imported** `request-mocking-protocol` via deep `dist/...` paths. Vite/Nitro still has to resolve those modules at build/dev time, and that package’s exports don’t line up with those paths, which produced the missing `./dist/request-matcher/utils.js` error. That was unrelated to whether the RMP branch ever ran.

### Fix (idiomatic local bypass)
1. **`org-api.ts`** — Removed all `request-mocking-protocol` usage. With `VITE_ORG_NEXT_BYPASS_AUTH=true`, server-side org API calls use **`resolveLocalOrgApiStub`** only (same as your existing in-memory fake data). Real deploys still use `fetch` + bearer token as before.
2. **`app-bootstrap.ts`** — If bypass auth is on, always use **`getFakeOrgNextAppBootstrap()`** (dropped the old “RMP vs stub” split).
3. **`org-next-flags.ts` / `vite-env.d.ts` / `.env.test`** — Removed **`VITE_ENABLE_RMP`** and **`isOrgNextRequestMockingEnabled`**.
4. **`vite-plugin-local-org-api.ts`** — With bypass auth, the dev middleware always serves the stub JSON for the org API paths (no special case that skipped middleware when RMP was on).
5. **`package.json`** — Removed **`request-mocking-protocol`** from org-next; **`pnpm install`** updated the lockfile.
6. **Playwright** — Dropped MockClient / `api-mocks.ts` (they depended on RMP). **`smoke.spec.ts`** now asserts against the **local stub** (`local@example.com`, **Local Org**, **Spring Membership Drive**). **`workspace-create.spec.ts`** was removed because it relied on per-request RMP mocks; you can bring it back later with a dedicated strategy (e.g. test-only reset of in-memory state + `workers: 1`) if you want that flow covered again.

### Local dev
Use the same as before: **`pnpm dev:local`** sets `VITE_ORG_NEXT_BYPASS_AUTH` and points the API client at the app origin so SSR and the browser both hit the stub (server functions + Vite middleware).

`pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p apps/org-next/tsconfig.json` passes. If you want, I can run `pnpm e2e` in your environment next (needs a full mise-activated shell with Node ≥ 25.3 per the repo).

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
No, this is not what we wanted. For local tests, we need to run using request mocking protocol to mock the client API, the API client. But when I'm running locally using `pnpm run dev`, I don't want to do any mocking, I want to actually hit my local database.

All of this was working before the latest file changes. You can look at the diff between the file changes and what is already checked in in the last commit and see what broke.

This should be the setup: mocking for tests, no mocking for local dev. For local dev, we're hardcoding the ID of the user that we want to fetch, bypassing the auth when we get bootstrap data, and just passing a hardcoded bear token for now.

On the backend for local dev, we've also bypassed the auth, so we don't even need the bear token right now. This is a temporary situation, but that's how we want it set up right now.
</user_query>

Comparing the repo to the last commit to restore dev (real API, auth bypass) vs tests (request-mocking-protocol for the API client).

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Here’s what was wrong and what we changed.

### What broke
Recent work mixed **two different “local” modes**:

1. **`pnpm dev` / `dev:local`** should talk to your **real org API** (local DB), with optional **OAuth bypass** + **fixed user id** + **dev bearer** — not the in-memory JSON stub.
2. **Playwright (`.env.test`)** should use **`request-mocking-protocol`** so SSR `fetch` can satisfy org API calls from **mock schemas** (`x-mock-request`), without a real backend.

The previous error came from **importing deep `request-mocking-protocol/dist/...` paths** that are **not** in the package `exports` map. The supported entry is **`request-mocking-protocol/fetch`**, which exposes `setupFetchInterceptor`.

### What we did

1. **`org-api.ts`** — Restored the **real `fetch` to `getServerOrgApiBaseUrl()`** with a **Bearer token** (session or dev). No in-memory stub on this path.

2. **`request-mocking-fetch-setup.ts`** (new) — When `VITE_ENABLE_RMP=true` and SSR, dynamically imports **`setupFetchInterceptor` from `request-mocking-protocol/fetch`** and wires it to TanStack’s **`getRequestHeaders()`** so each SSR `fetch` sees Playwright’s mock headers. **No deep `dist/` imports.**

3. **`org-api-access-token.ts`** (new) — `resolveOrgApiAccessToken()`: session token first; if `VITE_ORG_NEXT_BYPASS_AUTH=true`, use `VITE_ORG_NEXT_DEV_ACCESS_TOKEN` or **`""`** when your backend ignores auth.

4. **`app-bootstrap.ts`** — Matches the **last committed** shape again: loads **`/users`**, **`/tenants`**, **`/tenant_memberships`** with the resolved token and picks **`userId`** / **`tenantId`**.

5. **`org-next-flags.ts`** — `isOrgNextBypassAuth`, `isOrgNextRequestMockingEnabled`, **`getOrgNextDevUserId()`** (from `VITE_ORG_NEXT_DEV_USER_ID`).

6. **Removed** the Vite **fake org API middleware** and **`local-org-api-stub` / `fake-org-api-data`** so **`pnpm dev` never uses that stub.**

7. **`package.json`** — `dev:local` is again **`VITE_API_HOSTNAME=http://localhost:3000`** (aligned with the last commit). **`request-mocking-protocol`** is back under **devDependencies**.

8. **`.env.test`** — `VITE_ENABLE_RMP=true` and **`VITE_ORG_NEXT_BYPASS_AUTH=true`** so e2e can use a **placeholder token** while **RMP** supplies JSON (without wiring `sessionStorage` into the httpOnly session cookie). **`pnpm dev`** does **not** load `.env.test`.

9. **Playwright tests** — Restored **`MockClient`** + routing; smoke specs updated for the current UI (**`vendor`**, **“Your workspaces”**, login test when bypass doesn’t auto-redirect).

### How to run things

| Mode | Command / env | Behavior |
|------|----------------|----------|
| **Local dev, real API** | `pnpm dev` or `pnpm dev:local` with **no** `VITE_ENABLE_RMP` | Hits **`VITE_API_HOSTNAME`** (e.g. `http://localhost:3000`). Optionally set **`VITE_ORG_NEXT_BYPASS_AUTH`**, **`VITE_ORG_NEXT_DEV_USER_ID`**, **`VITE_ORG_NEXT_DEV_ACCESS_TOKEN`** in `.env`. |
| **E2E** | `playwright` loads **`.env.test`**: `VITE_ENABLE_RMP` + bypass for token | **RMP** mocks org API; no stub middleware. |

`pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p apps/org-next/tsconfig.json` passes.

If you want **`pnpm dev` without** setting env vars every time, we can add a **`.env.local`**-style example that only sets `VITE_ORG_NEXT_*` and keeps **`VITE_ENABLE_RMP` unset** — say if you want that committed pattern.

[REDACTED]
